itemListElement.name: AI Agents vs Spreadsheets: Which Tool Wins

mainEntity.name: When should I use an AI agent instead of a spreadsheet?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use an AI agent when the workflow involves multiple steps, decisions based on natural language, or pulling data from several systems. Use a spreadsheet when the calculation is well-defined, the data is structured, and the result is read by humans rather than triggered automatically.

mainEntity.name: How much does it cost to replace a spreadsheet with an AI agent?

acceptedAnswer.text: A simple agent that automates a recurring spreadsheet task typically runs $300-1,500 per month in API and infrastructure costs, plus 20-60 hours of one-time engineering. Compare that against the labor hours the spreadsheet currently consumes weekly.

mainEntity.name: Can AI agents make spreadsheets obsolete?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Spreadsheets remain the right tool for ad-hoc analysis, financial modeling with version control, and any workflow where a human needs to see the entire calculation. AI agents replace the repetitive tasks built around spreadsheets, not the spreadsheets themselves.
